jokes.text: Fifth Avenue is closed for a joint pain walkathon. It's only four blocks but they are so slow.
jokes.punchline: The joint pain walkathon participants are literally slow due to their condition
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'joint pain' affecting walking speed, well-structured with efficient delivery

jokes.text: Next stop, one millionth street and Central Park jogger memorial highway.
jokes.setup: Subway announcement going express for no reason
jokes.punchline: Absurd street numbers and dark memorial highway reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd fake subway announcement that captures NYC transit chaos with dark humor

jokes.text: # Three bucks, two bags, one me #
jokes.punchline: Bizarre song lyrics about mundane items
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist moment that captures NYC street performer energy

jokes.text: Say, where does a young prostitute get started in this town?
jokes.setup: Liz complaining about New York being overrated
jokes.punchline: Random innocent-sounding question about prostitution
jokes.explanation: Random shock humor that fits 30 Rock's style but lacks setup and character integration

jokes.text: we live in the world capital of culture, finance and King Kong attacks
jokes.punchline: Adding King Kong attacks to serious list of NYC attributes
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke with excellent economy, mixing serious with absurd in classic 30 Rock style

jokes.text: she is a thug in a cocktail dress. Alex Mccord, Real housewives of New York
jokes.setup: Liz quoting someone about New York
jokes.punchline: Citing Real Housewives as her source
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Liz's pop culture obsessions influencing her worldview

jokes.text: Now, technically that was about Luann being a bitch at Sonia's Art show.
jokes.setup: Liz getting deep into Real Housewives details
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining reality TV drama
jokes.explanation: Great character work showing Liz's obsessive knowledge of reality TV minutiae

jokes.text: Am I the only person who saw Obama's press conference on how to sneeze?
jokes.punchline: Reference to absurd presidential press conference topic
jokes.explanation: Absurd but believable political reference that captures both current events obsession and bureaucratic overreach

jokes.text: The gym said the odor was attracting bugs that were attracting bats that were attracting bat-hawks.
jokes.setup: Kenneth delivering Liz's smelly gym clothes
jokes.punchline: Escalating food chain of creatures attracted to the smell, ending with made-up 'bat-hawks'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation structure with perfect absurd endpoint in 'bat-hawks' - quintessential 30 Rock writing

jokes.text: Sam and Diane are the lesbian couple in my building who murdered each other.
jokes.setup: Reference to classic TV couple Sam and Diane
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're actually murderous lesbians in his building
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - innocent delivery of dark content, excellent misdirection from TV reference

jokes.text: It's in my contract that I only play blondes, non-Irish redheads, or bald sex robots.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and bizarre contract rider
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna vanity mixed with absurd specificity, great character integration

jokes.text: And if you take it away from me, I will kill myself. And then you.
jokes.setup: Jenna claiming drama is her thing
jokes.punchline: Threatening murder-suicide over being dramatic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna escalation with dark humor, excellent delivery structure

jokes.text: I won an Oscar, so now I get to do real art. Begins no dogs phase.
jokes.punchline: Declaring a 'no dogs phase' as artistic evolution
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy absurdism showing his weird artistic pretensions

jokes.text: Okay, I got in trouble the last time I said this, but maybe you were asking for it dressed like that.
jokes.punchline: Victim-blaming someone in a tuxedo
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Liz's tendency to say inappropriate things

jokes.text: How black was this dude? On a scale from Lisa Bonet to dotcom.
jokes.punchline: Creating an absurd racial scale using celebrities
jokes.explanation: Fits Tracy's character but relies on racial humor crutch

jokes.text: My assailant was a middle-aged white man wearing a button down shirt and dockers. Dockers!
jokes.punchline: Reveal of mundane middle-class attacker, emphasizing 'Dockers!'
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of expectations with Jack's horror at middle-class banality

jokes.text: His knife was from Eddie Bauer.
jokes.punchline: Even the weapon is from a mainstream outdoor retailer
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback escalation with brilliant economy and brand specificity

jokes.text: How could we pay their salaries without using their money?
jokes.setup: Jack explaining rich people are helping the poor
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about using poor people's money to pay them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack corporate logic twisted into absurd justification

jokes.text: It's Ghostbusters 2 all over again.
jokes.setup: Liz describing New York as a selfish filth-monster
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to the inferior Ghostbusters sequel
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits character but not particularly original

jokes.text: It's the original Ghostbusters all over again.
jokes.punchline: Jack countering with the superior original
jokes.explanation: Nice immediate callback showing character contrast through movie preferences

jokes.text: Concrete bunghole where dreams are made up. There's nothing you can do.
jokes.setup: Liz quoting Jay-Z about New York
jokes.punchline: Mangling the lyrics to be pessimistic and crude
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's negativity corrupting inspirational lyrics

jokes.text: What's a commercial?
jokes.punchline: Tracy doesn't know what a commercial is
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy out-of-touch moment with perfect economy

jokes.text: Hopefully it's not an important parg of my blurn.
jokes.setup: Liz sick and possibly having brain issues
jokes.punchline: Making up words due to illness affecting speech
jokes.explanation: Clever made-up words showing illness effects, very quotable

jokes.text: And his sexpectations will be high.
jokes.setup: Liz talking about Paul coming home
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'sex' and 'expectations'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but feels somewhat forced

jokes.text: Looser skin, same underwear.
jokes.punchline: Grim but efficient summary of aging
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy with dark humor about aging, very quotable

jokes.text: I can't order you a car unless it's after 9:00, or you have a note from your killer.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning note from killer as valid excuse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation delivered with corporate deadpan

jokes.text: I have a criminal skull shape.
jokes.setup: Confusion about Tracy helping with crime
jokes.punchline: Toofer's matter-of-fact self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Great character moment with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: I was part of a special task force of very young-looking cops who infiltrated high schools.
jokes.punchline: 21 Jump Street reference delivered seriously
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference that fits character but predictable

jokes.text: How do you do, fellow kids?
jokes.punchline: Delivering the meme line seriously
jokes.explanation: Relies on meme recognition rather than original humor

jokes.text: I get all my outfits at Ann Taylor loft. Their clothes fit my body and my budget.
jokes.punchline: Earnest endorsement of budget clothing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing class difference but not particularly clever

jokes.text: 40 grand a year. But don't let the woman who's blackmailing me know that.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning blackmail
jokes.explanation: Good character detail with dark humor casually delivered

jokes.text: if I sneeze, I'll do it like the president taught me.
jokes.punchline: Reference to earlier Obama sneeze conference
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier joke, shows Liz's obsessive rule-following

jokes.text: I'm pregnant with a kitty cat.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre pregnancy delusion
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist humor that works for sick delirium but lacks setup

jokes.text: I'm wearing an edible nightgown. It's breadstick flavor and it comes with a dipping sauce.
jokes.punchline: Specific food-themed lingerie with practical details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna absurdity with specific details that make it funnier

jokes.text: Like all the Thai STDs my penis is about to give you.
jokes.punchline: Comparing to spreading international STDs
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor fitting Paul's character but relies on shock value

jokes.text: The sex monkey I bought in Jakarta.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning exotic sex toy from travels
jokes.explanation: Fits Paul's worldly character but somewhat predictable shock humor

jokes.text: Like, Mr. Brady and tiger the dog?
jokes.punchline: Comparing to man-dog relationship from Brady Bunch
jokes.explanation: Clever absurd reference mixing TV families with inappropriate relationships

jokes.text: It's a whole new fetish called... Normalling.
jokes.setup: Jenna discovering they like normal couple behavior
jokes.punchline: Turning normalcy into a fetish with made-up term
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept perfectly fitting Jenna's character - turning normal into perverse

jokes.text: So last night, I napped on you and you liked it, you sick bitch.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining their normal behavior as fetish
jokes.punchline: Treating innocent cuddling as kinky with harsh language
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of the normalling concept with great contrast

jokes.text: And I put that Afghan all over you because I thought your feet might get chilly.
jokes.punchline: Describing considerate blanket placement as if it's kinky
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that maintains the absurd premise with specific domestic details

jokes.text: We go out as a couple to bed, bath and beyond and shop for home necessities. In front of everybody.
jokes.punchline: Treating mundane shopping as public perversion
jokes.explanation: Excellent continuation of normalling concept with specific retail reference

jokes.text: Well you make your hair every morning by sticking your head in a cotton candy machine.
jokes.setup: Jack arguing with Trump on phone
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd description of Trump's hair process
jokes.explanation: Great visual metaphor for Trump's hair with specific mechanism

jokes.text: where the starting salary is... $5 million a year.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly high starting salary for police
jokes.explanation: Good Jack out-of-touch moment showing his wealth perspective

jokes.text: Plaxico Burress just called our idea 'poorly thought out'. Then hung up by shooting his own phone.
jokes.punchline: Reference to Burress's gun incidents applied to phone hanging up
jokes.explanation: Clever application of real celebrity scandal to mundane action

jokes.text: Turkey, pastrami, Swiss, Russian dressing, Cole slaw and potato chips
jokes.setup: Liz wondering about getting a sandwich named after her
jokes.punchline: Overly specific and elaborate sandwich description
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's food obsession but overly detailed

jokes.text: Yes, a native American paradise!
jokes.setup: Jack talking about what New York once was
jokes.punchline: Tracy's unexpected historical interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Tracy's unexpected historical perspective

jokes.text: Slogan to come.
jokes.punchline: Admitting he hasn't thought of campaign slogan yet
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of grandiose moment with practical admission

jokes.text: Yeah, easy for the person who never does the dishes.
jokes.setup: Jenna saying vinyl placemats are dishwasher safe
jokes.punchline: Paul's passive-aggressive domestic complaint
jokes.explanation: Realistic couple bickering that supports normalling concept but not funny on its own

jokes.text: How many times have you climaxed? Zero. Me too.
jokes.setup: Jenna and Paul discussing their normalling experience
jokes.punchline: They track their sexual satisfaction with normal activities like perverts would
jokes.explanation: Good continuation that maintains the sexual framework for normal activities

jokes.text: Do you know anyone who wants to be called Daryl Weenus?
jokes.punchline: Absurd fake name on the list
jokes.explanation: Random name humor that's mildly amusing but lacks cleverness

jokes.text: Jack-Donaghy-is-running for-mayor-2013 new-York-this-is the-website.Com
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously long domain name because everything else was taken
jokes.explanation: Good modern observation about domain availability with Jack's overthinking

jokes.text: I also got you these goofy water balloons my mom's friend Ron used to always send me out to buy.
jokes.setup: Kenneth listing items he bought for Jack
jokes.punchline: Innocent description of clearly being sent to buy condoms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence about adult situation

jokes.text: I know they're condoms.
jokes.punchline: Immediate revelation he knows exactly what they are
jokes.explanation: Great immediate reversal of Kenneth's apparent innocence

jokes.text: All I had was a Chewbacca costume made out of used hair extensions.
jokes.setup: Tracy relating to Jack's fear after mugging
jokes.punchline: Specific gross costume he used for comfort
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and gross detail that perfectly fits Tracy's background

jokes.text: Let's go outside.
jokes.punchline: Jack's horrified reaction to going outside
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective moment showing Jack's fear through minimal dialogue

jokes.text: This morning, Paul and I signed up for zipcar. Now it's easier to get to Costco to stock up on cereal.
jokes.punchline: Treating mundane car sharing and bulk shopping as exciting
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of normalling theme with specific mundane details

jokes.text: discuss whether the chair would be better... Over by the window where there's more light?
jokes.punchline: Pete finishing her sentence with perfectly normal domestic discussion
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of Jenna's sex fantasy with Pete's mundane reality

jokes.text: That's just couple stuff.
jokes.setup: Jenna insisting it's a weird sex thing
jokes.punchline: Pete's matter-of-fact dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation with Pete's weary married perspective

jokes.text: Oh, you smell like when the stone Mountain tire fire joined up with the corpse fire, and then swept through the downtown Manure district.
jokes.setup: Kenneth commenting on Liz's terrible smell
jokes.punchline: Elaborate apocalyptic scenario of multiple fires creating the worst possible smell
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating description that builds to an absurdly specific awful smell

jokes.text: It was our fault for letting those high schoolers dance at their prom.
jokes.punchline: The cause was preventing teenagers from having prom
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth conservatism taken to absurd extreme - blaming dancing teens for disasters

jokes.text: A drawer full of leaky batteries that I don't know what to do with?
jokes.setup: Liz listing what following rules got her
jokes.punchline: Specific relatable household problem
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail that's both absurd and relatable

jokes.text: Plastic cups go on the top rack of the dishwasher so they don't melt. Otherwise, no rules.
jokes.punchline: Immediately stating a very specific rule
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contradiction showing Liz can't escape her rule-following nature

jokes.text: They keep you dry but it all has to go somewhere.
jokes.punchline: Explaining moisture-wicking fabric physics applied to body odor
jokes.explanation: Clever scientific explanation for gross humor that feels earned

jokes.text: Do you want me to go on my antibiotic rant? It's endless. You'll beg for death.
jokes.setup: Jack suggesting she take something for her cough
jokes.punchline: Liz warning about her own tendency to lecture
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware character moment showing Liz knows her flaws

jokes.text: Now, I don't cross lines, I just make 'em... disappear.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration with pause for effect
jokes.explanation: Good villain moment for Liz with appropriate dramatic flair

jokes.text: You know what, you and Jenna should come visit me and my wife in Montclair.
jokes.setup: Paul commenting on their normal behavior
jokes.punchline: Casual suburban couple invitation
jokes.explanation: Supports normalling theme but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Sexual walkabout.
jokes.setup: Jenna and Paul saying what they're thinking
jokes.punchline: Both arriving at same absurd relationship solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of their relationship arc with great term invention

jokes.text: Talking to myself to seem crazy... moo-goo!
jokes.setup: Liz checking off items for her plan
jokes.punchline: Self-aware crazy talk with made-up exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment showing Liz's calculated approach to seeming crazy

jokes.text: Gimme $20!
jokes.setup: Liz as the terrorizing subway criminal
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small amount demanded in mugging
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's modest criminal ambitions

jokes.text: The worst criminal in # N-Y-C
jokes.setup: Crowd cheering Jack's capture of Liz
jokes.punchline: Treating minor subway annoyance as major criminal
jokes.explanation: Good ironic treatment of Liz's minor crimes as major threat

jokes.text: You're the only woman I could ever fight to regain my manhood.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about fighting women
jokes.explanation: Good Jack moment mixing appreciation with subtle insult

jokes.text: You're the only woman I could ever fight to regain my manhood.
jokes.setup: Jack thanking Liz after tackling her
jokes.punchline: His gratitude is that she's masculine enough to fight without shame
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack backhanded compliment that insults while expressing genuine affection

jokes.text: 8 million people in this crazy, beautiful city, and I, Jenna Maroney, am going to go to town on every last one of them.
jokes.setup: Jenna's closing monologue about New York
jokes.punchline: Sexual threat against entire city population
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna ending mixing grandeur with sexual menace
